titleStmt.title: SII 41.198: original edition by S. Rajagopal
No. 198. (A.R. No. 416 of 1905.) Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District. On the north wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Gudimallur.

summary.p: Registers a gift of land with tax exemption to the temple of Kadāraṅgoṉḍa chōḻīśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyinār in Karaivaḻi-Valliyūr, a village of Kalavaippaṟṟu in Paḍuvūrkoṭṭam. It is said that as the channel from the river to the village Kandāpuri-pēṭṭai Tenkāl became useless (iṟantapaṭiyālē) some lands of the temple running through the flower garden were used to dig a new channel and for the same some lands were given to the temple in exchange.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . cakalaloka cakkaravatti śrīirācanārāyaṇaṉ campuvarāyaṟku yāṇṭu nālu Āvatu [jayaṅk]oṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattup paṭuvūrk koṭṭattuk kalavaippaṟṟuk karaivaḻi valliyūr
Uṭaiyār kaṭāraṅkoṇṭa coḷīcuramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku Ippaṟṟil karaivaḻi kantāpurippeṭṭait teṉkāl Ūravarom kalveṭṭik kuṭuttapaṭi Eṅkaḷ Ūr Āṟṟukkāl Iṟantapaṭiyāle Iṉṉāyaṉṉār tirunāmattukkāṇi
āṉa tirunantavaṉattukku naṭuve Āṟṟukkāl veṭṭukaiyil Iṉṉāyaṉārkku Eṅkaḷuril nañcey nilattil Āvāraṅkaraiyil puḷiyañceṟu kuḻi Irunūṟṟunāṟpatum tirunāmattukkāṇi Ākak kuṭuttu Iṉ
ṉilattai nokki varum kaṭamai poṉvari cūlavari Aricikāṇam maṟṟum Ippaṟṟil koḷḷum palavarikaḷum Upātikaḷum paḻavarikaḷum melpiṟakkum putuvarikaḷum Ūrviṉivokam Āṟṟukkāl Āḷamañci maṟṟumuḷḷavaiyum nāṅkaḷe Iṟuttuk koḷḷakkaṭavomākavum Iṉṉilattukku Āṟu(n)nir koṇṭu nirpāccik koḷḷak kaṭavarkaḷākavum Iṉṉila tāṅkaḷ veṇṭuṅkuṭi Amatti veṇṭina payir cetu
caṟuvamāniya Iṟaiili Āka cantirātittavarai naṭakkumpaṭi kalve
ṭṭi kuṭuttu tirunaṉtavanattukku Eṅkaḷ Āṟṟukālile Iṟaittu
koḷḷakaṭavarkaḷākavum viṭṭa nilattukku tiruccūlatāpanamum paṇṇi
kuṭuttu Āṟṟukālum veṭṭik koṇṭom peṭṭai teṉkāl
ūravarom Ippaṭikku Ivai Ūrkku camaintu Etirilicoḻa piramarāyan Eḻuttu śrīmāhecura Irakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 41.198 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
